title: Upon The Detracter
content:
I ask'd thee oft what poets thou hast read,
And lik'st the best?    Still thou repli'st, The dead.
I shall, ere long, with green turfs cover'd be;
Then sure thou'lt like, or thou wilt envy, me.
meaning:
"Upon The Detracter" is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
